citations.description: Fix: resolves HEAD to concrete SHA once at close-entry; threads through verify+sign unit
Drift guard uses the pinned concrete SHA ref, making _material_drifted a no-op for the fix path
Exhaustive test-suite search for test covering HEAD-drift in verify→sign window found zero matches

findings.criterion: A completion close whose HEAD moves to an UNRELATED commit between verify and sign does NOT produce a closed-without-signature ticket — either it pins the verified rev and signs it, or it aborts the close and leaves the ticket in_progress (retryable). Verified by a test that moves HEAD in the verify→sign window.
findings.detail: The code fix that pins HEAD to a concrete SHA at close-entry time IS present (transition_close.py lines 592–606: `ref = resolve_ref('HEAD', ...)` before `_completion_precheck`), and the drift guard at line 681 correctly re-resolves the same concrete SHA so the check is a no-op. However, the criterion EXPLICITLY requires 'a test that moves HEAD in the verify→sign window'. No such test exists anywhere in the test suite — exhaustive searches for TOCTOU, drift, close-ticket, HEAD-move, completion-race, and 4de6 in tests/ all returned no matches.

citations.description: Best-effort fallback: if resolve_ref fails, ref=None and the old TOCTOU path is active
Search for reverify-completion, sign-completion, resign_completion in src/ returned no matches
sign-review (for plan-review) exists as the analog; no equivalent for completion-verifier

findings.criterion: There is a non-`--force`, non-fabrication recovery to obtain the completion attestation for a closed-but-unsigned ticket (or the close never lands unsigned on a benign race in the first place).
findings.detail: No `reverify-completion`, `sign-completion`, or equivalent command exists (searches across src/ for reverify, resign_completion, sign_completion, resign-completion all returned no matches). The plan-review analog `rebar sign-review` / `resign_plan_review` was not extended to cover completion. The 'or' alternative (close never lands unsigned on a benign race) is satisfied for the PRIMARY scenario (pinned SHA prevents the race), but the best-effort fallback at lines 605–606 (`except Exception: ref = None`) still reverts to the old TOCTOU behavior where a race can produce an unsigned close — and in that case, no non-force recovery exists.

citations.description: Residual unsigned-close warning still says 'Re-close to certify' — a no-op for a closed ticket, does not mention rebar reopen

findings.criterion: The residual unsigned-close message (if any) names an ACTUALLY-WORKING recovery, not a no-op re-close.
findings.detail: The warning message at line 691 still reads: 'not attesting stale state. Re-close to certify against the current tree.' Since the ticket is already closed at this point, 'Re-close' is a no-op — the user would need to `rebar reopen` first, which the message does not mention. This message still fires on the fallback path (when resolve_ref fails and ref falls back to None). It is unchanged from the broken message described in the ticket.

citations.description: No test for HEAD-drift in verify→sign window found in tests/ — searches for TOCTOU/drift/completion-race/4de6 in tests/ returned no relevant matches

findings.criterion: make lint && make typecheck && make test green.
findings.detail: Cannot run the tools, but criterion 1 explicitly requires a test that does not exist in the repository. Without that test, `make test` cannot be green for the scenario this ticket addresses. The test suite was exhaustively searched and no test covering the verify→sign HEAD-drift window (the primary regression) was found.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
